title: Onobrychis supina
intro: Onobrychis supina, also known as the smooth milk-vetch or smooth bird's-foot, is a perennial legume plant native to Asia and Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help alleviate conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Onobrychis supina include isoflavones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Onobrychis supina is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can also be found in the form of capsules or tinctures, often used to treat menstrual disorders and promote bone health.
taxonomy: Onobrychis supina is classified as a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and relationships to other plants within the family. Onobrychis supina is further classified as a member of the subfamily Faboideae and the tribe Hedysareae. Within this tribe, it belongs to the genus Onobrychis, which comprises a group of legume plants. As a member of the Onobrychis genus, Onobrychis supina shares similarities with other species in the genus in terms of its morphology and growth habits.
uses: Onobrychis supina, also known as the smooth bird's-foot trefoil, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of certain diseases. The plant is said to be used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems. Additionally, its extracts have been studied for their potential to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Onobrychis supina.
benefits: Onobrychis supina, also known as the sand splet, is a type of legume with potential health benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its effects. properties: Onobrychis supina has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and research has shown that it possesses various therapeutic properties. constituents: Onobrychis supina, also known as the smooth bird's-foot or low milkvetch, contains a variety of bioactive compounds. These active constituents include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which have been reported to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. parts: Onobrychis supina is primarily used as a medicinal herb, and the parts that are considered medicinal are the leaves and flowers. The leaves and flowers are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems. preparations: Onobrychis supina is known to be used in traditional medicine, where it is often used to make herbal preparations for treating various health issues.
